checklist_item.title: Verify Trust Boundary Integrity: Is decode_transaction the inverse of encode_transaction?
checklist_item.test_procedure: 1. For each transaction type, create valid transaction object. 2. Encode transaction to bytes. 3. Decode bytes back to transaction object. 4. Verify decoded == original for all fields. 5. Test round-trip for adversarial field values.
checklist_item.notes: Source: PROP-0040. Boundary: tb-004. Non-bijective codec enables transaction manipulation.

checklist_item.title: Verify Trust Boundary Integrity: Does decode_transaction reject unknown type prefixes?
checklist_item.test_procedure: 1. Send bytes with type prefix 0x05 and verify TransactionTypeError. 2. Test all invalid prefixes (0x05-0xFF). 3. Verify no silent acceptance of unknown types. 4. Verify proper error propagation (not IndexError, etc.). 5. Check boundary between RLP legacy and typed transactions.
checklist_item.notes: Source: PROP-0041. Boundary: tb-004. Unknown type acceptance causes undefined behavior.

checklist_item.title: Verify Trust Boundary Integrity: Is decode_transaction crash-resistant to malformed input?
checklist_item.test_procedure: 1. Fuzz decode_transaction with random byte sequences. 2. Verify only TransactionTypeError or valid transaction returned. 3. Check no IndexError, OverflowError, MemoryError propagates. 4. Test truncated inputs, oversized inputs, malformed RLP. 5. Verify node stability under adversarial P2P traffic.
checklist_item.notes: Source: PROP-0042. Boundary: tb-004. Crash from P2P packet is critical DoS vulnerability.

checklist_item.title: Verify Trust Boundary Integrity: Are EIP-7702 authorizations bound to chain_id and nonce?
checklist_item.test_procedure: 1. Create EIP-7702 authorization with specific chain_id and nonce. 2. Verify signing_hash_7702 includes chain_id and authorization data. 3. Attempt replay with different chain_id - verify rejection. 4. Attempt replay with stale nonce - verify rejection. 5. Verify nonce must match current authorizer account nonce.
checklist_item.notes: Source: PROP-0043. Boundary: tb-008. Fusaka-related EIP-7702. Stale auth replay enables unauthorized code delegation.

checklist_item.title: Verify Trust Boundary Integrity: Is transaction hash computation deterministic and unique?
checklist_item.test_procedure: 1. Compute hash for legacy transaction: keccak256(rlp.encode(tx)). 2. Compute hash for typed transaction: keccak256(type_prefix || tx_data). 3. Verify same transaction produces same hash across multiple calls. 4. Verify different transactions produce different hashes. 5. Test for hash collisions across transaction types.
checklist_item.notes: Source: PROP-0044. Boundary: tb-001. Non-deterministic or colliding hashes break tx tracking.

checklist_item.title: Verify Trust Boundary Integrity: Does close_state release all references?
checklist_item.test_procedure: 1. Call close_state after block finalization. 2. Verify _main_trie reference deleted. 3. Verify _storage_tries reference deleted. 4. Verify _snapshots reference deleted. 5. Verify created_accounts reference deleted. Check memory profile in long-running node.
checklist_item.notes: Source: PROP-0045. Boundary: tb-003. Memory leak affects long-running nodes.

checklist_item.title: Verify Trust Boundary Integrity: Does set_account_balance set (not add) the balance?
checklist_item.test_procedure: 1. Set account balance to value X. 2. Call set_account_balance with value Y. 3. Verify balance == Y (not X + Y). 4. Test with coinbase payment code paths. 5. Verify no ether inflation from incorrect set semantics.
checklist_item.notes: Source: PROP-0046. Boundary: tb-003. Add instead of set causes ether inflation.

checklist_item.title: Verify Trust Boundary Integrity: Does set_code trigger empty account destruction?
checklist_item.test_procedure: 1. Create account with only code (nonce==0, balance==0). 2. Call set_code with empty bytes. 3. Verify modify_state destroys the now-empty account. 4. Test with CREATE/CREATE2 deployment that fails. 5. Test EIP-7702 code delegation scenarios.
checklist_item.notes: Source: PROP-0047. Boundary: tb-003. Ghost accounts if destruction not triggered.

checklist_item.title: Verify Trust Boundary Integrity: Is EIP-155 signing hash correctly formed?
checklist_item.test_procedure: 1. Compute signing_hash_155 for test transaction. 2. Verify hash == keccak256(rlp.encode([...fields, chain_id, 0, 0])). 3. Verify trailing zeros are included. 4. Attempt cross-chain replay of EIP-155 transaction. 5. Verify different chains produce different signing hashes.
checklist_item.notes: Source: PROP-0048. Boundary: tb-009. Broken EIP-155 enables cross-chain replay attacks.

checklist_item.title: Verify Trust Boundary Integrity: Is ECDSA non-repudiation property maintained?
checklist_item.test_procedure: 1. Verify signing hash includes all transaction fields. 2. Verify no field can be modified without invalidating signature. 3. Verify transaction hash provides unique permanent identifier. 4. Review for any signing hash construction flaws. 5. This is primarily a cryptographic property verification.
checklist_item.notes: Source: PROP-0049. Boundary: tb-001. Non-repudiation violations require ECDSA breaks (theoretical).

checklist_item.title: Verify Conditional Scope: Are Fusaka gas economics consensus-safe under transaction ordering?
checklist_item.test_procedure: 1. Verify TX_MAX_GAS_LIMIT bounds single transaction gas. 2. Verify calldata_floor is enforced regardless of ordering. 3. Test block building with various transaction orderings. 4. Compare results across client implementations. 5. Document MEV implications but note out-of-scope.
checklist_item.notes: Source: PROP-0050. CONDITIONAL SCOPE - MEV generally out-of-scope unless causing consensus failure.

checklist_item.title: Verify Trust Boundary Integrity: Does Engine API enforce full transaction validation?
checklist_item.test_procedure: 1. Verify JWT authentication on Engine API endpoints. 2. Submit invalid block payload via Engine API. 3. Verify full EL transaction validation occurs. 4. Verify invalid transactions are rejected despite CL trust. 5. Test that compromised CL cannot bypass EL validation.
checklist_item.notes: Source: PROP-0051. Boundary: tb-006. CL-EL boundary. Invalid block processing causes consensus failure.

checklist_item.title: Verify Trust Boundary Integrity for tb-009: Does get_beacon_proposer_index correctly use pre-computed proposer_lookahead instead of on-demand computation?

reachability.notes: If this function falls back to on-demand computation instead of using the lookahead, it would produce different results (since state may have changed since lookahead computation), causing consensus failures.

checklist_item.test_procedure: 1. Locate the get_beacon_proposer_index function implementation. 2. Verify it accesses state.proposer_lookahead[state.slot % SLOTS_PER_EPOCH] directly. 3. Confirm there is NO fallback path to on-demand computation (compute_proposer_index or equivalent). 4. Create a test case where state changes between epoch boundary and slot execution. 5. Compare the proposer index returned by lookahead-based method vs. on-demand computation to verify they could diverge. 6. Verify all code paths through get_beacon_proposer_index use the lookahead array exclusively.
checklist_item.notes: Source: PROP-W1B10-36, Trust Boundary: tb-009, Invariant: FULU-SG005-INV-001. EIP-7917 modification requires strict use of pre-computed lookahead.

checklist_item.title: Verify Consensus Layer correctly populates proposer_lookahead at epoch boundaries

reachability.notes: Supporting check for actor-consensus-layer node coverage.

checklist_item.test_procedure: 1. Identify where proposer_lookahead is computed and stored in state. 2. Verify computation occurs at epoch boundaries only (not per-slot). 3. Confirm the lookahead array has exactly SLOTS_PER_EPOCH entries. 4. Verify the computation uses the correct state snapshot (epoch boundary state). 5. Check that proposer_lookahead is immutable within an epoch.
checklist_item.notes: Source: PROP-W1B10-36, Supporting node: actor-consensus-layer

checklist_item.title: Verify Validator uses correct proposer index from lookahead for block proposal duties

reachability.notes: Supporting check for actor-validator node coverage.

checklist_item.test_procedure: 1. Trace how validators determine their block proposal duties. 2. Verify validators query get_beacon_proposer_index which uses the lookahead. 3. Confirm validators do not perform independent on-demand computation. 4. Check that validators and consensus layer agree on proposer assignment for any given slot. 5. Test edge cases at epoch boundaries where lookahead transitions.
checklist_item.notes: Source: PROP-W1B10-36, Supporting node: actor-validator

checklist_item.title: Verify Trust Boundary Integrity: Is fork upgrade irreversibility enforced across all state transitions at tb-007?

reachability.notes: Fork irreversibility is consensus-critical. A reversible fork would allow fork oscillation attacks.

checklist_item.test_procedure: 1. Identify all code paths that invoke upgrade_to_fulu(state). 2. Verify that post-state always has fork.current_version == FULU_FORK_VERSION after upgrade. 3. Enumerate all state transition functions and verify none can revert fork.current_version to a pre-Fulu version. 4. Attempt to craft P2P messages that could trigger fork version reversion. 5. Check for race conditions or edge cases in fork scheduler that could cause fork oscillation. 6. Verify that beacon node rejects any state with inconsistent fork version history.
checklist_item.notes: Source: PROP-W2B3-3, Trust Boundary: tb-007, Invariant: fork-SG-001-INV-003. Fork oscillation attacks could cause network instability and consensus failure.

checklist_item.title: Supporting Check: Does actor-fork-scheduler correctly enforce one-way fork transitions?

reachability.notes: Fork irreversibility is consensus-critical. A reversible fork would allow fork oscillation attacks.

checklist_item.test_procedure: 1. Review fork scheduler logic for fork version ordering enforcement. 2. Verify that fork scheduler only permits forward transitions (e.g., DENEB -> FULU, never FULU -> DENEB). 3. Check that fork epoch boundaries are correctly calculated and enforced. 4. Test edge cases around epoch boundaries where fork transitions occur. 5. Verify scheduler rejects any attempt to schedule a downgrade.
checklist_item.notes: Source: PROP-W2B3-3, Supporting node: actor-fork-scheduler

checklist_item.title: Supporting Check: Does actor-beacon-node validate fork version monotonicity in received states?

reachability.notes: Fork irreversibility is consensus-critical. A reversible fork would allow fork oscillation attacks.

checklist_item.test_procedure: 1. Review beacon node state validation logic for fork version checks. 2. Verify that beacon node rejects any state where fork.current_version is less than expected for the given epoch. 3. Test P2P sync scenarios where a malicious peer sends states with reverted fork versions. 4. Check that state transitions within beacon node maintain fork version monotonicity. 5. Verify that checkpoint sync and snap sync correctly validate fork versions.
checklist_item.notes: Source: PROP-W2B3-3, Supporting node: actor-beacon-node

checklist_item.title: Can upgrade_to_fulu corrupt or drop BeaconState fields during Electra-to-Fulu fork transition?

reachability.notes: State preservation during fork is consensus-critical. Triggered automatically at fork epoch via P2P consensus.

checklist_item.test_procedure: 1. Enumerate all fields in electra.BeaconState and identify which fields are explicitly modified by upgrade_to_fulu (expected: fork, proposer_lookahead). 2. Create a pre-fork BeaconState with known values for all fields. 3. Execute upgrade_to_fulu and capture the post-state. 4. For each field NOT in the explicit modification list, assert post.F == pre.F. 5. Check for subtle corruption vectors: (a) deep-copy vs shallow-copy issues for nested structures, (b) default value initialization that overwrites existing data, (c) missing field assignments in new struct initialization, (d) type conversion errors during SSZ serialization/deserialization. 6. Verify field preservation under edge cases: maximum-size validators list, full historical roots, boundary epoch values.
checklist_item.notes: Source: PROP-W2B3-4, Invariant: fork-SG-002-INV-001, Assumption: asm-009. This is consensus-critical - any field corruption would cause chain splits or fund loss.

checklist_item.title: Falsification Test: Can the fork version history chain be broken during upgrade_to_fulu transition?

reachability.notes: Incorrect version chaining would cause P2P fork digest mismatches.

checklist_item.test_procedure: 1. Identify the upgrade_to_fulu state transition function in the beacon node codebase. 2. Examine the implementation to verify that post.fork.previous_version is explicitly set to pre.fork.current_version. 3. Design test cases that exercise the upgrade transition with various pre-states, including edge cases (e.g., genesis fork, consecutive upgrades). 4. Verify the post-condition holds: assert(post.fork.previous_version == pre.fork.current_version). 5. Attempt to falsify by: (a) checking for any code path that could skip the version assignment, (b) testing with corrupted pre-state fork data, (c) verifying no race conditions exist in concurrent state transitions. 6. Validate that fork digest computation downstream correctly uses the maintained version chain.
checklist_item.falsification_strategy: Attempt to trigger upgrade_to_fulu with edge-case pre-states (empty version, mismatched versions, repeated upgrades) and verify whether the version chain invariant can be violated. Check for off-by-one errors in version array indexing.
checklist_item.impact_if_violated: Fork digest mismatch causing P2P network partition - nodes with broken version chains would compute different fork digests and fail to communicate with correct nodes.
checklist_item.notes: Source: PROP-W2B3-5, Invariant: fork-SG-002-INV-002, Assumption: asm-009. Bug bounty eligible with PoC showing fork version chain breakage.

checklist_item.title: Does upgrade_to_fulu correctly set post.fork.current_version to FULU_FORK_VERSION?

reachability.notes: Wrong fork version causes complete P2P network isolation.

checklist_item.test_procedure: 1. Locate the upgrade_to_fulu function in the state transition code. 2. Trace the assignment of post.fork.current_version to verify it uses FULU_FORK_VERSION constant. 3. Verify FULU_FORK_VERSION constant matches the expected fork version from the specification. 4. Create a test case that invokes upgrade_to_fulu and asserts post.fork.current_version == FULU_FORK_VERSION. 5. Test edge cases: verify the upgrade path from the previous fork (Electra) correctly transitions to Fulu. 6. Attempt to falsify by checking if any code path could set an incorrect version or skip the version assignment.
checklist_item.notes: Source: PROP-W2B3-6, Post-condition from invariant fork-SG-002-INV-003. Incorrect fork version after upgrade causes the node to become incompatible with the network, effectively creating a network partition. This is a critical post-condition that must hold after every fork upgrade operation.

checklist_item.title: Falsification Test: Can the fork epoch in post-state diverge from the epoch at which upgrade_to_fulu was triggered?

reachability.notes: Incorrect fork epoch in state would affect epoch-dependent logic.

checklist_item.test_procedure: 1. Identify the upgrade_to_fulu function in the state transition code. 2. Trace how post.fork.epoch is assigned during the upgrade. 3. Verify that get_current_epoch(pre) is correctly computed and assigned to post.fork.epoch. 4. Design test cases where pre-state has edge-case epoch values (epoch 0, max epoch, boundary epochs). 5. Attempt to trigger upgrade_to_fulu at different points in the epoch lifecycle to verify consistency. 6. Check for any code paths that could set fork.epoch to a different value than the current epoch. 7. Verify that no intermediate state modifications could cause epoch drift between reading pre-state and writing post-state.
checklist_item.notes: Source: PROP-W2B3-7, Post-condition for fork-SG-002-INV-004. This is an internal property requiring formal verification approach. Focus on proving the post-condition cannot be violated through any execution path of upgrade_to_fulu.

checklist_item.title: Can the proposer_lookahead field be incorrectly initialized during the Fulu fork upgrade, causing wrong proposer assignments?
checklist_item.test_procedure: 1. Identify the upgrade_to_fulu function in the beacon chain state transition code. 2. Verify that initialize_proposer_lookahead(pre) is called with the correct pre-state. 3. Compare post.proposer_lookahead against the expected output of initialize_proposer_lookahead(pre). 4. Test edge cases: empty validator set, maximum validator count, validators at boundary indices. 5. Verify no stale data from pre-state leaks into the new proposer_lookahead. 6. Check that proposer scheduling after the fork uses the correctly initialized lookahead. 7. Simulate a fork transition and verify proposer assignments match expectations.
checklist_item.notes: Source: PROP-W2B3-8, Invariant: fork-SG-002-INV-005, Assumption: asm-017. Post-condition verification for EIP-7917 proposer lookahead initialization during Fulu fork. Incorrect initialization could enable unauthorized block proposals by assigning wrong proposers.

checklist_item.title: Is the upgrade_to_fulu function pure and free of pre-state side effects?

reachability.notes: Pre-state mutation would cause non-deterministic behavior but is hard to exploit externally.

checklist_item.test_procedure: 1. Identify the upgrade_to_fulu function implementation. 2. Create a deep copy of the pre-state before calling upgrade_to_fulu. 3. Execute upgrade_to_fulu with the pre-state. 4. Compare the original pre-state copy with the pre-state after execution - they MUST be identical. 5. Check for any in-place mutations of state fields, particularly mutable data structures. 6. Verify that the function returns a new state object rather than modifying the input. 7. Test with edge cases: empty states, maximum-size states, and states at fork boundaries.
checklist_item.notes: Source: PROP-W2B3-9, Invariant: fork-SG-002-INV-006. CONDITIONAL SCOPE: Requires further investigation to determine full bug bounty eligibility. Pre-state mutation during fork would cause non-deterministic state transitions, potentially causing consensus splits.

checklist_item.title: Falsify Fork Version Branch Exclusivity: Can compute_fork_version return ambiguous or missing results for any epoch value?

reachability.notes: Used in fork digest computation for P2P topic subscription.

checklist_item.test_procedure: 1. Identify all branch conditions in compute_fork_version that determine which fork version is returned based on epoch. 2. Extract the epoch thresholds for each fork (e.g., GENESIS_EPOCH, ALTAIR_FORK_EPOCH, BELLATRIX_FORK_EPOCH, CAPELLA_FORK_EPOCH, DENEB_FORK_EPOCH, ELECTRA_FORK_EPOCH). 3. Test boundary epoch values: exactly at each fork epoch threshold, one below, one above. 4. Verify branch conditions are mutually exclusive: check that no epoch value satisfies multiple branch conditions simultaneously. 5. Verify branch conditions are exhaustive: check that every valid epoch value (0 to MAX_UINT64) satisfies exactly one branch condition. 6. Look for off-by-one errors in comparison operators (< vs <=, > vs >=). 7. Test with epoch = 0 (genesis) to ensure it returns GENESIS_FORK_VERSION. 8. Test with epoch = MAX_UINT64 to ensure the latest fork version is returned. 9. Verify the ordering of if-else branches matches the chronological order of forks to prevent earlier branches from shadowing later ones.
checklist_item.notes: Source: PROP-W2B3-11, Invariant: p2p-SG-001-INV-002. Violation would cause P2P network fragmentation where nodes on different fork versions cannot communicate, effectively partitioning the network.
